news.headline: Intel Rises 3% on Q2 Earnings Beat, Upbeat Q3 Outlook as Chip Sector Stays Flat
news.summary: Intel's stock is surging while the rest of the chip sector sits flat, and the reasons behind that split tell a much bigger story about where the semiconductor race is actually headed.

gamePlan: Bias into the open: Mixed — watch only — wait for confirmation.
Signals are conflicted — let the first 30 minutes set the tone before committing risk.
Use QQQ $685.10 as the risk-on/off line; trade with it, not against it.
Primary idea: TSLA PUT — enter $12.90–$14.26, target $20.37+, stop $8.15.
Keep total options exposure modest; one A+ setup beats five marginal ones.
report.narrative: Futures point to a softer open with QQQ -0.99% and SPY +0.08%. Composite sentiment -5 (Mixed). The tape is being driven mainly by qqq / mega-cap tech, vix, sector rotation. VIX at 14.45 (-1.1%). Environment read: watch only — wait for confirmation. Healthcare (+1.00%) is leading while Financials (-1.82%) lags. Premarket strength in AAPL, LLY; weakness in COIN, AMD. The desk's highest-conviction setup is TSLA (7.0/10).
report.riskWarning: Options trading is risky and can result in the loss of the entire premium paid. This report provides analysis and educational information only — not financial advice. Size positions small (1–2% of capital per idea), honor stops, and do your own research.
